# [bug] ask_knowledge_base returns empty synthesis for type='src'/'ai-infrastructure' queries despite correct references

## Symptom

`ask_knowledge_base` with `type='src'` or `type='ai-infrastructure'` returns placeholder text in the `answer` field (`"I don't have enough information..."` / `"provided context documents are empty"`) while the `references` array comes back populated with high-scoring, correctly-indexed source files. The same query with `type='all'` (default) synthesizes a full, grounded answer from the same underlying corpus.

## Reproducers

**Case 1 — `type='src'` (empty answer, populated refs):**
```
ask_knowledge_base({query: 'How does the IssueSyncer sync GitHub issues to local markdown files?', type: 'src'})
# answer: "I don't have enough information from the provided documents..."
# references: [src/core/Base.mjs, src/component/Base.mjs, src/main/addon/Base.mjs, ...]
```

**Case 2 — `type='ai-infrastructure'` (same symptom):**
```
ask_knowledge_base({query: 'refetchIssuesByNumber detectStaleCommentsCounts exhaustTimelineItems', type: 'ai-infrastructure'})
# answer: "I don't have enough information to answer your question, as the provided context documents are empty."
# references: [
#   IssueSyncer.mjs (ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/services/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs, score 793),
#   IssueService.mjs (ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/services/IssueService.mjs, score 635),
#   DreamService.mjs (ai/daemons/DreamService.mjs, score 341), ...
# ]
```

**Control — `type='all'`, same query (full synthesis):**
```
ask_knowledge_base({query: 'refetchIssuesByNumber detectStaleCommentsCounts IssueSyncer GitHub sync'})
# answer: Full synthesis naming IssueSyncer, FETCH_ISSUES_FOR_SYNC, the comment-sync flow
# references: [src/core/Base.mjs, ai/mcp/.../IssueSyncer.mjs, Updater.md, GitHubWorkflow.md, ...]
```

## What's NOT the bug

- **Indexing is not broken.** `ApiSource.mjs:42-49` sourceMap includes `'ai': 'ai-infrastructure'`. References prove the chunks are in the collection (with content-appropriate scores). `type='all'` synthesizes correctly using those same chunks.

## Suspected Root Cause

Very likely in `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/QueryService.mjs` or `DocumentService.mjs` — the path that feeds chunk content to the synthesis LLM. Hypotheses to investigate:
1. Type-filtered Chroma queries return only metadata + references (not chunk `document` content), so synthesis gets empty strings.
2. `SourceParser`-emitted chunks store content under a different metadata key than `LearningSource`-emitted chunks, and the synthesis path reads only one.
3. A recently-changed filter clause strips `document` field when a `type` filter is present.

## Impact

Per AGENTS.md §2.1 Anti-Hallucination Tool Hierarchy, `ask_knowledge_base` is the PRIMARY tool for conceptual + implementation questions. Agents filtering by `type='src'` for code-level questions silently get useless placeholder answers despite the KB having the content — a latent value-defeat of the RAG layer. Discovered during the #10092 ticket-intake verification sweep.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified and documented in the fix (QueryService.mjs or sibling)
- [ ] `ask_knowledge_base` with `type='src'` returns a real synthesis matching `type='all'` quality
- [ ] `ask_knowledge_base` with `type='ai-infrastructure'` returns a real synthesis
- [ ] Playwright spec that queries with `type='src'` AND `type='ai-infrastructure'` for a known-indexed method signature; asserts the answer references the method name or nearby context (not placeholder "empty" text)
- [ ] No regression on `type='all'` / `type='guide'` synthesis

## Origin Session ID
`d9eb5e76-5430-45f7-b3ea-8600664d28f9`

Related: Discovered during #10092 / PR #10093 verification.


## Timeline

- 2026-04-19T12:25:24Z @tobiu added the `bug` label
- 2026-04-19T12:25:24Z @tobiu added the `ai` label
- 2026-04-19T12:25:24Z @tobiu added the `architecture` label
- 2026-04-19T12:25:34Z @tobiu assigned to @tobiu
- 2026-04-19T12:28:13Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2ec752d` - "fix(knowledge-base): resolve relative source paths in SearchService synthesis (#10097)

ask_knowledge_base was returning placeholder "I don't have enough
information" answers for every type='src' and type='ai-infrastructure'
query despite references coming back correctly populated. Users could see
the KB had retrieved the right files but the synthesis LLM kept saying
the documents were empty.

Root cause: SearchService.ask passed `ref.source` directly to
`fs.pathExists` and `fs.readFile` without resolving against
`neoRootDir`. LearningSource emits ABSOLUTE paths (worked fine).
ApiSource emits RELATIVE paths like
`ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/services/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs` — these
resolved against the MCP server process's CWD, not the neo repo, so
`fs.pathExists` returned false and content fell through to
`No Content (File missing or empty)`. The synthesis prompt then
contained placeholder text for every source-type chunk and Gemini
correctly reported empty documents.

Fix: resolve `ref.source` against `aiConfig.neoRootDir` before the
existence check. `path.isAbsolute()` short-circuits for already-absolute
paths (LearningSource remains unchanged). Added a warn log on fallback so
future silent content-delivery regressions are visible.

Playwright spec covers three cases: relative-path resolution succeeds,
absolute paths still work (no regression), and genuinely-missing files
fall through cleanly with the warn log.

Impact: restores ask_knowledge_base's value proposition for
implementation questions (Anti-Hallucination §2.1 — the PRIMARY tool for
conceptual + code-level understanding). Agents filtering by type='src'
for framework-internal questions now get real grounded answers instead
of placeholder refusals."
- 2026-04-19T12:28:44Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10098
- 2026-04-19T12:33:58Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2402e2a` - "fix(knowledge-base): normalize ApiSource to emit absolute source paths (#10097)

Complements the SearchService defensive resolve from the previous commit by
addressing the root cause upstream. ApiSource was the sole outlier among
eight source loaders (LearningSource, TicketSource, DiscussionSource,
PullRequestSource, ReleaseNotesSource, TestSource, ApiSource itself) —
every other loader emits metadata.source resolved against neoRootDir,
while ApiSource passed relativeEntryPath (e.g.
'ai/mcp/server/.../IssueSyncer.mjs') to SourceParser. That inconsistency
is what silently defeated ask_knowledge_base synthesis for type='src'
and type='ai-infrastructure' queries.

Change: ApiSource.indexRawDirectory now passes the already-computed
absolute entryPath to SourceParser.parse instead of relativeEntryPath.
One-line fix. The absolute fullPath was already used for fs.readFile on
the preceding line, so this aligns emission with internal usage.

Also updated the SearchService comment to reframe the defensive resolve
as a safety net for future loader drift rather than the primary fix.

Note: the existing Chroma collection still contains relative-path chunks
from prior syncs. A knowledge-base re-sync (action: 'sync') will rewrite
those chunks with absolute paths, but meanwhile the SearchService
resolve-before-read continues to work transparently for both shapes."
- 2026-04-19T12:42:01Z @tobiu referenced in commit `3f78740` - "fix(knowledge-base): normalize all source loaders to emit neoRootDir-relative paths (#10097)

Reverses the direction from the previous commit. Absolute paths in the
distributed Chroma zip would bake tobi's local filesystem layout into
every neo release, breaking ask_knowledge_base for every recipient but
him. The zip is a first-class release artifact — portability is
non-negotiable.

Corrected direction: ALL source loaders now store metadata.source as a
path relative to neoRootDir. SearchService resolves against the
consumer's own neoRootDir at read time. The distributed zip works
identically on every machine.

Changes (reverts + forward fixes):
- ApiSource: reverts to passing relativeEntryPath to SourceParser
  (undoes commit 2402e2a96 — the direction was wrong).
- LearningSource: passes path.relative(neoRootDir, filePath) to
  DocumentationParser instead of the absolute filePath.
- DiscussionSource, TicketSource, PullRequestSource, ReleaseNotesSource:
  switch their chunk.source emission from absolute filePath to
  path.relative(neoRootDir, filePath). These were previously baking
  author-local absolute paths into the zip — breakage had already been
  shipping for non-author recipients.
- SearchService: comment rewritten to reflect portability rationale —
  relative-source storage is the contract, resolve-before-read is the
  primary mechanism (not a safety net). The path.isAbsolute short-circuit
  preserves backward compatibility with legacy absolute-path chunks in
  existing Chroma collections until recipients re-sync.

TestSource was already using relativeEntryPath and is unchanged.
ApiSource was also correct originally; my earlier absolute-path commit
misdiagnosed the architectural intent and is fully reverted here.

Spec re-runs clean (3/3 at 555ms) — the regression tests exercise both
absolute (legacy) and relative (new) shapes and continue to hold."
- 2026-04-19T12:46:12Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-04-19T12:46:12Z @tobiu referenced in commit `e94e2c3` - "fix(knowledge-base): resolve relative source paths in SearchService synthesis (#10097) (#10098)

* fix(knowledge-base): resolve relative source paths in SearchService synthesis (#10097)

ask_knowledge_base was returning placeholder "I don't have enough
information" answers for every type='src' and type='ai-infrastructure'
query despite references coming back correctly populated. Users could see
the KB had retrieved the right files but the synthesis LLM kept saying
the documents were empty.

Root cause: SearchService.ask passed `ref.source` directly to
`fs.pathExists` and `fs.readFile` without resolving against
`neoRootDir`. LearningSource emits ABSOLUTE paths (worked fine).
ApiSource emits RELATIVE paths like
`ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/services/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs` — these
resolved against the MCP server process's CWD, not the neo repo, so
`fs.pathExists` returned false and content fell through to
`No Content (File missing or empty)`. The synthesis prompt then
contained placeholder text for every source-type chunk and Gemini
correctly reported empty documents.

Fix: resolve `ref.source` against `aiConfig.neoRootDir` before the
existence check. `path.isAbsolute()` short-circuits for already-absolute
paths (LearningSource remains unchanged). Added a warn log on fallback so
future silent content-delivery regressions are visible.

Playwright spec covers three cases: relative-path resolution succeeds,
absolute paths still work (no regression), and genuinely-missing files
fall through cleanly with the warn log.

Impact: restores ask_knowledge_base's value proposition for
implementation questions (Anti-Hallucination §2.1 — the PRIMARY tool for
conceptual + code-level understanding). Agents filtering by type='src'
for framework-internal questions now get real grounded answers instead
of placeholder refusals.

* fix(knowledge-base): normalize ApiSource to emit absolute source paths (#10097)

Complements the SearchService defensive resolve from the previous commit by
addressing the root cause upstream. ApiSource was the sole outlier among
eight source loaders (LearningSource, TicketSource, DiscussionSource,
PullRequestSource, ReleaseNotesSource, TestSource, ApiSource itself) —
every other loader emits metadata.source resolved against neoRootDir,
while ApiSource passed relativeEntryPath (e.g.
'ai/mcp/server/.../IssueSyncer.mjs') to SourceParser. That inconsistency
is what silently defeated ask_knowledge_base synthesis for type='src'
and type='ai-infrastructure' queries.

Change: ApiSource.indexRawDirectory now passes the already-computed
absolute entryPath to SourceParser.parse instead of relativeEntryPath.
One-line fix. The absolute fullPath was already used for fs.readFile on
the preceding line, so this aligns emission with internal usage.

Also updated the SearchService comment to reframe the defensive resolve
as a safety net for future loader drift rather than the primary fix.

Note: the existing Chroma collection still contains relative-path chunks
from prior syncs. A knowledge-base re-sync (action: 'sync') will rewrite
those chunks with absolute paths, but meanwhile the SearchService
resolve-before-read continues to work transparently for both shapes.

* fix(knowledge-base): normalize all source loaders to emit neoRootDir-relative paths (#10097)

Reverses the direction from the previous commit. Absolute paths in the
distributed Chroma zip would bake tobi's local filesystem layout into
every neo release, breaking ask_knowledge_base for every recipient but
him. The zip is a first-class release artifact — portability is
non-negotiable.

Corrected direction: ALL source loaders now store metadata.source as a
path relative to neoRootDir. SearchService resolves against the
consumer's own neoRootDir at read time. The distributed zip works
identically on every machine.

Changes (reverts + forward fixes):
- ApiSource: reverts to passing relativeEntryPath to SourceParser
  (undoes commit 2402e2a96 — the direction was wrong).
- LearningSource: passes path.relative(neoRootDir, filePath) to
  DocumentationParser instead of the absolute filePath.
- DiscussionSource, TicketSource, PullRequestSource, ReleaseNotesSource:
  switch their chunk.source emission from absolute filePath to
  path.relative(neoRootDir, filePath). These were previously baking
  author-local absolute paths into the zip — breakage had already been
  shipping for non-author recipients.
- SearchService: comment rewritten to reflect portability rationale —
  relative-source storage is the contract, resolve-before-read is the
  primary mechanism (not a safety net). The path.isAbsolute short-circuit
  preserves backward compatibility with legacy absolute-path chunks in
  existing Chroma collections until recipients re-sync.

TestSource was already using relativeEntryPath and is unchanged.
ApiSource was also correct originally; my earlier absolute-path commit
misdiagnosed the architectural intent and is fully reverted here.

Spec re-runs clean (3/3 at 555ms) — the regression tests exercise both
absolute (legacy) and relative (new) shapes and continue to hold."
